title: URIs, URL Resolution, Origins, DNS, and Destination Identity · Go Client Systems · Runtime Contract: Dns Address Port
summary: A release mirror accepts a configurable upstream URL and must prove the canonical origin, DNS answer, certificate name, and connected destination before download. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go dns address port increment in a Go 1.26 net/http destination-resolution and origin contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace a hostname through DNS answers, address selection, route, port, connection, and certificate identity. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Compare origins from scheme, host, and effective port while separating URI authority, Host, and connection address. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
